Need to send email from Node.js? Express + Nodemailer walkthrough with two Gmail approaches — OAuth2 (safer but more setup) and App Password (simple credentials).
Sending Gmail with nodemailer After Google Killed Password Auth (OAuth 2.0)

This time I want to add an extra note about nodemailer. Google no longer lets you send Gmail messages with just a username and password, so I'm writing down how to set up Express + OAuth 2.0 + nodemailer + Gmail instead. It's a quick walkthrough — building the OAuth client, grabbing the refresh and access tokens, and wiring them into nodemailer.
Sending Email in Node.js with Nodemailer (Including Gmail)

Email is one of those features that shows up everywhere — subscriptions, bookings, sign-ups, you name it. So in this post I'm jotting down how to send email from a Node.js app using Nodemailer, including how to wire it up with Gmail.
